{"id":"project-LICHEN-7yu8","title":"hkdf_expand uses truncating cast for counter byte","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/oscore/hkdf.c
Lines: 101, 134

The HKDF expand function uses uint16_t for counter but casts to uint8_t when encoding:

```c
uint16_t counter = 1;
// ...
buf[buf_len++] = counter++;  // Implicit truncation to uint8_t
```

Per RFC 5869, the counter is a single-byte value (1-255). The okm_len check at line 107 ensures we never exceed 255*32 bytes output, so counter never exceeds 255 iterations. The code is correct, but the implicit truncation could be clearer:

```c
buf[buf_len++] = (uint8_t)counter++;
```

This is a style/clarity issue, not a bug. The comment says 'uint16_t to avoid overflow if bounds check is ever weakened' which is good defensive coding.

Severity: Low - code is correct but implicit truncation should be explicit for readability and to avoid compiler warnings with -Wconversion.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:50:32.56562Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:50:32.565833Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-7yu8","title":"hkdf_expand uses truncating cast for counter byte","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/oscore/hkdf.c
Lines: 101, 134

The HKDF expand function uses uint16_t for counter but casts to uint8_t when encoding:

```c
uint16_t counter = 1;
// ...
buf[buf_len++] = counter++;  // Implicit truncation to uint8_t
```

Per RFC 5869, the counter is a single-byte value (1-255). The okm_len check at line 107 ensures we never exceed 255*32 bytes output, so counter never exceeds 255 iterations. The code is correct, but the implicit truncation could be clearer:

```c
buf[buf_len++] = (uint8_t)counter++;
```

This is a style/clarity issue, not a bug. The comment says 'uint16_t to avoid overflow if bounds check is ever weakened' which is good defensive coding.
